case_title: RALPH E HIGGS VS. EOLA MELANCON et al

docket_entries.description: CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF HIGGS, RALPH E ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 4.0 DAYS
MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-20-2009 10:30 AM FOR DEPT 612

DISCOVERY 612, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSE TO INTEROGATORIES COMPELLING RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DECUMENTS; THAT THE TRUTH OF ALL MATTERS IN PLTFS' FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS BE ADMITTED BECAUSE DEFT HAS NOT SERVED SWORN TIMELY RESPONSES AND FOR SANCTIONS AND ATTORNEYS FEES - NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF RALPH E. HIGGS'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES, TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND THAT REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS BE DEEMED ADMITTED; AND FOR SANCTIONS IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. THE NOTICE WAS INSUFFICIENT PER C.C.P. SECTIONS 1005 AND 1013. THE SIXTEENTH COURT DAY IS OCTOBER 20, 2009, AND THE MOTION WAS SERVED BY EXPRESS MAIL. THE FIRST DAY THE MOTION COULD HAVE BEEN HEARD IS OCTOBER 22, 2009. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

DISCOVERY 612, RE-NOTICE OF MO & MO FOR ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES CCP 2030 COMPELLING RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS CCP 2023(B); 2031(L) THAT THE TRUTH OF ALL MATTERS IN PLTF'S FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS BE ADMITTED BECAUSE DEFT HAS NOT SERVED SWORN TIMELY RESPONSES AND FOR SANCTIONS, AND ATTY FEES - PLAINTIFF RALPH E. HIGGS' MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES, RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, THAT THE TRUTH OF ALL MATTERS IN PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS BE ADMITTED IS GRANTED. DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF RALPH HIGGS' MOTION FOR CONTEMPT OF ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND COMPELLING RESPONSES TO DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS AN ORDER THAT DEFENDANT EOLA MELANCON'S ANSWER AND HER TWENTY-EIGHT AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BE STRICKEN AS A TERMINATING SANCTIONS. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO RESPOND AND TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. THE COURT FINDS THAT ANOTHER ORDER TO RESPOND WOULD NOT RESULT IN COMPLIANCE. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order For Contempt Of Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Compelling Responses To Documents And For Sanctions And Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF RALPH HIGGS' Motion For Contempt Of Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Compelling Responses To Documents is granted as it seeks an order that Defendant Eola Melancon's Answer and her twenty-eight affirmative defenses be stricken as a terminating sanctions. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond and to comply with the court's prior order were a misuse of the discovery process. The court finds that another order to respond would not result in compliance. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant in Propria Persona for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar and dissolved/fee paid. Proceedings not reported. (212)

Re-Notice Of Mo & Mo For Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories Ccp 2030 Compelling Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents Ccp 2023(B); 2031(L) That The Truth Of All Matters In Pltf'S First Set Of Requests For Admissions Be Admitted Because Deft Has Not Served Sworn Timely Responses And For Sanctions, And Atty Fees
Off Calendar Nov-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Ralph E. Higgs' Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories, Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents, That The Truth Of All Matters In Plaintiff's First Set Of Request For Admissions Be Admitted is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond to Plaintiff's Interrogatories and Demand For Production Of Documents within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's First Set Of Request For Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $600, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Response To Interogatories Compelling Responses To Demand For Production Of Decuments; That The Truth Of All Matters In Pltfs' First Set Of Requests For Admissions Be Admitted Because Deft Has Not Served Sworn Timely Responses And For Sanctions And Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Oct-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Ralph E. Higgs's Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories, To Compel Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents, And That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; And For Sanctions is ordered off calendar. The notice was insufficient per C.C.P. Sections 1005 and 1013. The sixteenth court day is October 20, 2009, and the motion was served by express mail. The first day the motion could have been heard is October 22, 2009. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-18-2009 Set For Trial On Apr-05-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Mar-16-10 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 502
